body.title: Fix: main.py uses wrong logger name — diagnostics silently discarded
body.description:
main.py uses `_logging.getLogger(__name__)` where __name__=wild_igor.igor.main. logging_setup.py only registers handlers for the igor.* hierarchy (cognition.log, tools.log, etc). wild_igor.igor.main is outside this hierarchy → all INFO/WARNING from main.py's inline diagnostic log calls (web_server.send attempt, bare-ack suppressed, web-source turn produced empty response, etc.) are silently discarded.

Fix: change all `_logging.getLogger(__name__)` in main.py to `logging.getLogger('igor.main')` so they route to master.log (WARNING+) and console (INFO+).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/main.py (3 getLogger(__name__) call sites)
**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — fix logger name at 3 inline call sites (lines 8024, 8030, 8041, 8057). OUT — logging_setup.py changes, other logger name issues elsewhere.
**Test plan:** grep confirms no remaining `_logging.getLogger(__name__)` in main.py's web-reply path; manual trace confirms 'web_server.send attempt' would now appear in cognition.log or master.log.
